title: 'The presence of PFAS in my blood is out of my control': Six Europeans on living in 'forever pollution' hotspots
subhead: Being a victim is neither a profession nor their identity. Meta, Cristina, Doris, Stéphanie, Mari and Toon live in PFAS contamination hotspots in six European countries.

title: Investigators denied access to Elysée Palace in events contracts probe
Israeli envoy says 'on the same side' with Lebanon after talks in US
Behind the war in Iran, Trump's obsession with global energy resources
subhead: The international decisions of the US president have baffled analysts. Yet he has proven highly predictable in his attraction to territories rich in natural resources, whether Greenland, Venezuela, or Iran.

title: 'The Strait of Hormuz crisis shows that the seas are no longer spaces of freedom, but territories whose control comes at a price'
subhead: The Iranian regime's grip on this narrow stretch of sea demonstrates, conversely, the value of international law when it is respected by all, contrary to what Donald Trump believes, writes Le Monde journalist Julien Bouissou in his column.

title: The devastated residents of Chibab Tower, struck by four Israeli missiles
subhead: Israel targeted Ali Yusuf Harshi, the nephew and secretary of Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem. The attack killed 10 people in this 1970s apartment building, which stood as a symbol of Lebanon's ability to 'live together.'

title: New online search tool in Germany reveals if ancestors were Nazi Party members
subhead: In partnership with American and German archives, the weekly 'Die Zeit' has launched a searchable tool granting access to more than 12 million documents, cataloging the bulk of Nazi Party (NSDAP) memberships from 1925 to 1945.

American Catholics outraged at Trump's attack of pope and AI-generated image of himself as Christ
subhead: The US president claimed, against all plausibility, that the image he posted on social media showed him as a doctor, but he was forced to take it down. The post, along with his lengthy tirade against Leo XIV, left believers stunned.

title: Will California's next governor be a Republican?
subhead: The crowded field of Democratic candidates running in the gubernatorial election raises the possibility that a Trump-aligned Republican could win in November in this deeply progressive state.

title: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
subhead: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.
